reason: Response B exhibits stylistic hallmarks consistent with Claude Haiku: short punchy sentences, deliberate brevity, self-aware commentary about being a small/fast model, and a tone that is direct without being verbose. It also reflects on its own nature as a "quick" model in a way that would be natural for Haiku specifically. Response A, by contrast, reads more like a larger model (e.g., Sonnet) reflecting on its tool-rich Claude Code context with longer, more elaborate paragraphs and meta-commentary about the orchestration environment it was placed in.
